title: Unchecked ERC20 arithmetic lets arbitrary callers drain approved-none balances
claim: The contract uses Solidity 0.7.0 without checked arithmetic. In the ERC20 branch of `transferFrom`, `allowance[from][msg.sender]` is decremented with raw subtraction and never validated first. When allowance is zero or too small, it underflows instead of reverting, and execution continues into `_transfer`, which also performs unchecked balance arithmetic.
impact: Any account holding fungible balance can have that balance stolen without approval as long as the attacker requests an amount that does not trigger the later burn-loop failure. Liquidity pool balances are equally exposed, enabling direct theft of tradable funds.
paths: Call `transferFrom(victim, attacker, amount)` with `amount > minted` so the ERC20 path is taken, while `allowance[victim][attacker] == 0`.
Line 255 underflows the allowance instead of reverting.
If `victim` has at least `amount`, `_transfer` moves the tokens to the attacker and updates balances with no authorization check ever succeeding.

title: Transfer gate becomes a honeypot by permanently blocking sells to the chosen pool address after 50 blocks
claim: `_preTransferCheck` records the first transfer recipient as `_uniswapV3Pool` and, once `_disableTransferBlock` expires 50 blocks later, reverts every transfer to that address unless the sender is whitelisted. The deployer is whitelisted at construction, so privileged addresses can still exit while ordinary holders cannot.
impact: If the chosen address is the trading pool, non-whitelisted users can buy but are later unable to sell back into liquidity, creating a direct honeypot and permanent fund lock for public holders.
paths: The first transfer that sets `_uniswapV3Pool` points it at the launch liquidity pool or any other chosen recipient.
After `block.number > _disableTransferBlock`, any `transfer`, `transferFrom`, or `safeTransferFrom` to that address by a non-whitelisted holder reverts with `Transfers are disabled to sell tokens`.
Whitelisted insiders retain the ability to move or sell while non-whitelisted buyers are trapped.

title: Buyers only get a two-block window to sell back to the pool
claim: Whenever tokens are received from `_uniswapV3Pool`, the recipient's `initialBuyBlock` is stored. Any later transfer back to `_uniswapV3Pool` reverts once more than two blocks have passed since that recorded buy.
impact: Even before the broader 50-block sell freeze activates, ordinary buyers effectively have only a two-block exit window. Holding longer makes their position unsellable into the pool, which can trap user funds almost immediately after purchase.
paths: A user buys from the designated pool, setting `initialBuyBlock[user] = block.number`.
After waiting at least three blocks, the user attempts to transfer tokens back to the pool.
`_preTransferCheck` reverts with `Transfers are disabled after 2 block of initial buy`.
